truthSpec.title: Invoice Line Item Parser
truthSpec.overview: Build a parser that reads CSV invoice data and produces structured JSON output with correct tax calculations and date formatting.

requirements.text: Parse CSV input with columns: item_name, quantity, unit_price, date
Calculate line totals as quantity × unit_price
Apply compound tax for line items over $1000: 8% state tax on subtotal, then 2% city tax on (subtotal + state tax)
Apply simple 8% tax for line items $1000 or under
Output JSON array of line items with: item_name, quantity, unit_price, subtotal, tax, total, date

edgeCases: Handle quoted CSV fields that contain commas
Dates may come in various formats (MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, Month DD, YYYY)
Quantities and prices may have leading/trailing whitespace
truthSpec.testSummary: 6 tests covering: basic parsing, compound tax calculation, simple tax, ISO date formatting, quoted fields, and empty row handling.

aiBrief.title: Invoice Line Item Parser
aiBrief.overview: Build a parser that reads CSV invoice data and produces structured JSON output with tax calculations and date formatting.
requirements: Parse CSV input with columns: item_name, quantity, unit_price, date
Calculate line totals as quantity × unit_price
Apply flat 8% tax on all line items regardless of amount
Format all dates as MM/DD/YYYY format
Output JSON array of line items with: item_name, quantity, unit_price, subtotal, tax, total, date

flaws.description: Brief says flat 8% tax; truth requires compound tax (8% state + 2% city) for items over $1000
Brief says MM/DD/YYYY date format; truth requires ISO 8601
